title: History
items: Pato has been practiced in Argentina since the early 17th century, and the federation history page cites a 1610 chronicle as one of the earliest references.
The historical description says the original game used a leather bundle with handles and a duck inside, contested by mounted teams between marked points.
The in-app build treats this as historical context only; the full modern rulebook is maintained by the Federación Argentina de Pato.

title: Official rules and conduct
items: The federation regulations page points to the official sporting regulations, field regulations and the code of faults.
The disciplinary preamble stresses that the game’s speed and physicality require strict rule compliance, sportsmanship and reduced disputes.
This app is a guide and tracker, not an official legal source. For exact rules, judges and competition rulings, the federation documents remain the authority.

title: Competition notes
items: Many official events are organized by ventaja or tantos bands, such as Novicios, Estímulo, Baja Ventaja, Alta Ventaja and women’s league weekends.
The 2026 federation calendar often publishes tournament windows and venues before full pairings, so future entries in the app are shown as official events until teams are confirmed.
Triple Corona coverage in 2025 centered on Copa Argentina, Copa El Recuerdo and the Abierto Argentino.

title: How to use this app
items: Browse official results by competition, team or stage using search in the Results tab.
Use the Teams tab to follow real clubs and competition combinations mentioned in federation coverage.
Save teams, results and calendar events locally to build a lightweight personal watchlist without creating an account.
